Group:18889633.name: PyRVA
Group:18889633.welcomeBlurb:
Welcome to PyRVA! 

We can't wait to __init__() you to the group.  We currently meet at the University of Richmond campus every second Wednesday of the month.  Topics vary monthly for a broad range of users and/or skills.  We would greatly appreciate any feedback or requests for topics.  We're also open to requests/recommendations for speakers. 

Please feel free to share the group information with anyone that might be interested in Python.

We look forward to meeting you.

PyRVA.org 
(http://www.pyrva.org) - Visit site to join the Slack channel.

ProNetwork:610554310475497472.name: Python Software Foundation Meetup Pro Network

GroupQuestion:8939666.question: What do you want to learn?

GroupQuestion:9273714.question: What is your biggest struggle right now with python?

Event:309617696.title: Down the rabbit hole: Using Python for text searches and indexing
Event:309617696.description:
In this hands-on session, Rob Harrison and Monty Shepardson will walk you through a Python program that "indexes" a narrative by matching a passage from Alice in Wonderland against a curated keyword list.

Watch how the script scans the text, adapts to changes in length or composition, and tallies keyword occurrences with practical flair.

Bring your curiosity! After the demo we'll open the floor for a discussion about alternative approaches and enhancements. What opportunities will the audience bring up to make their script better?

Whether you're a coding novice or a seasoned developer, you'll pick up ideas and inspiration.

**Schedule**
5:45 pm - Doors Open / Social
6:30 pm - Meeting Start
7:30 pm - Wrap-up
8:00 pm - Prologue (Social in Scotts Addition)

**All events are open to all skill levels**. If you're just starting out, come to learn and feel free to ask questions. That's where the real learning happens. If you're a more seasoned developer, we'd love to learn from you. Despite what the Zen of Python says, there are usually more than one way to do something, so share your knowledge!

Join our Discord server! **https://pyrva.org/discord**
Want to sponsor PyRVA? **https://pyrva.org/donate**
Want to present? **https://forms.gle/q8w3xziArWjud5f67**

Event:311199623.title: Quantum computing with Python
Event:311199623.description:
Quantum computing has become a popular topic recently.

Qiskit, a Python library for quantum computing, allows you to both simulate quantum computer operations or send it to be run on a real quantum computer.

Quantum computing is used to solve some calculations that would take a regular computer a very long time to calculate or would otherwise be uncomputable. Applications include scientific computations, simulations, and the most infamous, breaking encryptions.

Come join us as we explore the quantum future!

**Schedule**
5:45 pm - Doors Open / Social
6:30 pm - Meeting Start
7:30 pm - Wrap-up
8:00 pm - Prologue (Social in Scotts Addition)

**All events are open to all skill levels**. If you're just starting out, come to learn and feel free to ask questions. That's where the real learning happens. If you're a more seasoned developer, we'd love to learn from you. Despite what the Zen of Python says, there are usually more than one way to do something, so share your knowledge!
